hero.hero_title_1: Run Any Database Natively on Red Hat OpenShift - Without the Compromise.
hero.hero_description: Deploy and manage 25+ Production-grade databases as native Kubernetes resources on Red Hat OpenShift. Run production databases with full automation, governance, and portability across on-prem, cloud, edge, and air-gapped environments.

what_this_means.heading: What This Means for Red Hat OpenShift Users
what_this_means.description: If you're running applications on Red Hat OpenShift, you benefit from enterprise Kubernetes with enhanced security and developer experience. KubeDB extends these benefits to your database layer with native operator patterns.
what_this_means.subtitle: KubeDB enhances OpenShift by making databases first-class citizens in your platform:

items.title: Faster Time-to-Value
items.description: Launch databases in minutes with OpenShift Operators.

items.title: Enterprise Compliance
items.description: Meet regulatory requirements with OpenShift security.

items.title: Hybrid Flexibility
items.description: Run on-prem, cloud, or edge with consistent workflows.

items.title: VM & Container Support
items.description: Run databases on OpenShift Virtualization or containers.

platform.heading: Production databases. Fully automated on Red Hat OpenShift.
platform.description: 25+ Database engines with HA, scaling, backup, TLS, monitoring, and GitOps support

cards.title: Run Databases Natively on Red Hat OpenShift
cards.description: KubeDB integrates deeply with Red Hat OpenShift, supporting OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF), CSI drivers, and both containerized and virtualized workloads. Databases run with full security context constraints and enterprise-grade isolation.

cards.title: Enterprise-Grade Security & Compliance
cards.description: Complete data ownership with Red Hat OpenShift's security features including SELinux, seccomp, and Pod Security Admission. Support for air-gapped, FIPS-140-2, and highly regulated environments.

cards.title: Broad Database Support
cards.description: Manage 25+ Database engines including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and more, all with consistent Kubernetes-native workflows on both containers and VMs.
cards.note: KubeDB fills the gap - turning MKE into a full-featured, open database platform.

cards.title: Automated Lifecycle Management
cards.description: Declarative operators handle provisioning, upgrades, scaling, and repair with zero-downtime where possible.

cards.title: High Availability & Resiliency
cards.description: Multi-zone clustering, automated failover, PodDisruptionBudgets, and continuous health checks with Red Hat OpenShift Operator Hub integration.

cards.title: Backup & Recovery
cards.description: Integrated with KubeStash for policy-driven, incremental, encrypted backups to S3-compatible storage. Support for point-in-time recovery.

cards.title: Monitoring & Observability
cards.description: Native Prometheus + Grafana dashboards. Integrates with Red Hat OpenShift monitoring stack and Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management.

cards.title: Security & Governance
cards.description: Automated TLS, Transparent Data Encryption, RBAC integration with Red Hat OpenShift OAuth, network policies, and Red Hat Compliance Operator support.

cards.title: Legacy Workloads
cards.description: Run databases on Red Hat OpenShift alongside legacy Virtualized workloads, Windows applications, or Bare Metal deployments.

comparison.heading: KubeDB on MKE delivers what traditional DB management can't
comparison.description: KubeDB delivers broader support, lower cost, and true Kubernetes-native experience on your existing OpenShift platform.

rows.traditional: Limited (often 5–10 engines, vendor-specific like Postgres/MySQL via certified Operators)
rows.kubedb: 25+ engines including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch, ClickHouse, Cassandra, Neo4j & more

rows.traditional: Console-driven or external service with limited Red Hat OpenShift integration
rows.kubedb: Native CRDs via Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM). Full declarative GitOps

rows.traditional: Per-core / usage licensing + egress fees for external DBaaS
rows.kubedb: No licensing fees. Pay only for your Red Hat OpenShift nodes + storage

rows.traditional: Minutes to hours, often manual approval
rows.kubedb: Seconds via kubectl / GitOps. Developer self-service
Native ODF Ceph CSI - high performance, snapshots, erasure coding
Built-in TLS/encryption + OpenShift RBAC, SELinux, NetworkPolicy
Deep integration with OpenShift Monitoring + Prometheus
KubeStash integration + ODF snapshots, point-in-time recovery
Same manifests across any Red Hat OpenShift or CNCF certified Kubernetes

architecture.heading: KubeDB runs natively on Red Hat OpenShift
architecture.description: The KubeDB Operator sits directly on top of your OpenShift platform, leveraging OpenShift's enterprise security, operator framework, and multi-cluster capabilities.

layers.label: KubeDB Platform
layers.desc: Web Console • Envoy Gateway • Hub/Spoke Model • Policy Engine

layers.label: Kubernetes Services
layers.desc: ArgoCD • Open Cluster Management • Cert Manager • Prometheus • Grafana

layers.label: Red Hat OpenShift
layers.desc: OCP • ROSA • ARO • OSD • OKE • OCP+
